replies.author: @Blisterdude123
replies.text:
The thing is, I was totally fine with Cap giving him the shield. I thought it was a lovely moment, and earned. Cap lost his best friend Bucky (before Winter Soldier etc), and Sam Wilson became his friend in the new world he found himself in, totally alone.
I think Bucky should still have become the new Captain America, but I was always okay with the gesture of Cap giving Sam the shield. It read to me as his way of thanking him for being his friend, after he'd lost everything else, waking up in the future. It should have been presented like Cap passing the torch of who he was and splitting it between his 'two' heirs. Bucky for the title, and Sam with the item that defined him. There's no way Cap would've expected anyone other than Bucky to be able to 'be' Captain America, since he could take the physical punishment in a way Sam couldn't.
Bucky shoud've taken up the mantle, but Sam keeping the shield made sense to me.

Some dudes are just set-in-stone as their Marvel Characters and it will be almost impossible to recast them for any near future generations.  Iron Man, Thor, and Captain America are the Big 3 that would be really hard to recast for this or the next generation of moviegoers. replies.author: @victorcates9330
replies.text:
If they can pivot to a new generation, then they can avoid resetting the storyline. However, there's always a tomorrow. Even if the new superheroes were all successful, do they just hand off their mantles gain in 10 years? Unless an actor aging out means permanent retirement of the character, they've have to eventually recast and reset. The same problem would presumably apply if one of the new generation is crazy successful. There would be the question of bringing back Steve Rogers, and Iron Man, and then whether you bring back the replacements to merge generations - which has the same problem of recasting.

If you have a problem that you could face today or tomorrow, then you might as well face it today. A reset is clean. Each poor decision adds baggage. It seems a weird task to just keep asking the MCU to drag the weight or more and more mistakes. What's the exit strategy on the current narrative? Plow forward until you accept complete financial collapse?

replies.author: @Blisterdude123
replies.text:
The thing is, I was totally fine with Cap giving him the shield. I thought it was a lovely moment, and earned. Cap lost his best friend Bucky (before Winter Soldier etc), and Sam Wilson became his friend in the new world he found himself in, totally alone.
I think Bucky should still have become the new Captain America, but I was always okay with the gesture of Cap giving Sam the shield. It read to me as his way of thanking him for being his friend, after he'd lost everything else, waking up in the future. It should have been presented like Cap passing the torch of who he was and splitting it between his 'two' heirs. Bucky for the title, and Sam with the item that defined him. There's no way Cap would've expected anyone other than Bucky to be able to 'be' Captain America, since he could take the physical punishment in a way Sam couldn't.
Bucky shoud've taken up the mantle, but Sam keeping the shield made sense to me.

text:
Black isn’t a super power, but everything falcon does with the shield equal to or better than Steve rogers implies it.  How can it not?  You’re pitting an ordinary guy against a hulk.  A HULK.  Even Steve rogers would lose that fight.

It’s weird.  I can accept the black widow picking up cap’s shield from her motor cycle, going a hundred miles an hour (which means the shield hit her hands at that speed), but I can’t accept that an ordinary guy can land into the ground at an instant stop from what may as well be a hundred miles an hour.  I can’t accept that he can use his wings to cut thru an automobile like that.  Falcon’s charm was that he was an ordinary guy with plum luck and faithfulness in the cap.  That was taken away from him in the tv show to give him an arc.

Shelve marvel and Star Wars for 10 years, shake loose all the fake hanger ons.  Start over.
